properties.notes:
**Batch 1,2,3 - Scenario 37: Deceased Estate - COVID-19 Extension Period**

Property was deceased's main residence. Beneficiary sold within extended period due to COVID-19 delays.

**Timeline:**
- 20 Nov 2002 to 15 Apr 2021: Main residence (deceased) - 18.4 years
- 15 Apr 2021: Death of owner - property passes to beneficiary
- 15 Apr 2021 to 20 Feb 2024: Deceased estate period (2.85 years)
- Sale occurred 10 months beyond normal 2-year limit

**Key Rules:**
- Normal two-year exemption: 15 Apr 2021 to 15 Apr 2023
- COVID-19 extension available for delays in selling
- PCG 2021/4 provides additional time for COVID-affected estates
- Sale delays attributable to COVID-19 lockdowns and market conditions
- **NO CGT PAYABLE** - Extended deceased estate exemption applies

**Expected Result:**
- NO CGT PAYABLE - COVID-19 extended deceased estate exemption (PCG 2021/4)
- Beneficiary's cost base: Market value at death ($785,000)
- Capital gain (before exemption): $17,125
- Full exemption under extended provisions

**ATO Rules Applied:**
- Section 118-195 ITAA 1997 (Deceased Estate Exemption)
- PCG 2021/4 (COVID-19 Extension for Deceased Estates)
- Commissioner exercises discretion for COVID-affected sales
- Section 128-15 ITAA 1997 (Market value cost base for beneficiary)
